subject: Statistics
title: Active Controls vs. Placebos
description: A micro-lesson that makes Active Controls vs. Placebos usable.

hook.text: Is it ethical to give a cancer patient a sugar pill when a proven treatment exists? Often, no.

buildup.text: Active-control trials compare a new treatment to the best existing one — not to nothing.

discovery.text: Non-inferiority trials ask: is the new drug at least as good as the current standard? Not necessarily better.

twist.text: A drug that's equally effective but has fewer side effects is a genuine advance — even without superiority.

climax.text: Choosing placebo vs. active control is both a statistical and ethical decision. Both matter.

punchline.text: Sometimes 'no worse' is good enough. The comparison group shapes the question.

quiz.question: When are active-control trials used instead of placebo-controlled trials?
options: When a proven treatment exists and withholding it from patients would be unethical
